questions.questionText: The middle ear cavity is connected to the nasopharynx via:
questions.optionB: Eustachian tube (auditory tube — connects middle ear to nasopharynx, equalizes middle ear pressure, opens during swallowing/yawning)
questions.explanation: Core concept: Eustachian tube connects middle ear to nasopharynx for pressure equalization. Why correct: Eustachian tube: opens in nasopharynx (Rosenmüller's fossa nearby). Functions: ventilation/pressure equalization, drainage of secretions, protection from nasopharyngeal sounds. In children: shorter, wider, more horizontal → predisposes to otitis media. Why A wrong: IAM — transmits CN VII, VIII from posterior fossa to inner ear. Why C wrong: EAC connects pinna to tympanic membrane (external ear). Why D wrong: Cochlear aqueduct — connects cochlea to subarachnoid space (perilymph communication). Clinical relevance: Eustachian tube dysfunction → negative middle ear pressure → serous otitis media (glue ear). In children: adenoid hypertrophy blocks Eustachian opening. Exam trap: Children's ET anatomy (short, wide, horizontal) = MORE infections. Adults (long, narrow, 45°) = LESS infections. Memory aid: 'Eustachian = Equalizes pressure between Ear and nasopharynx.' Reference: Dhingra – Ear Anatomy.
questions.conceptTag: Eustachian tube middle ear nasopharynx pressure
